17

18

20

25

INCHES

7

27 28 29

443

6775

The Quebec Gazette.

GEORGE PREVOST.

[N°. 2551.]

Gazette de Québec.

JEUDI LE 6 JANVIER, 1814.

THURSDAY, JANUARY 6, 1814. MAY next, at TEN of the clock in the forenoon, at which WHEREAS, the Co-partnership heretofore subsistingbet- time and place the conditions of sale will be made known. FREDk. W. ERMATINGER, Sheriff FrederickM'Kenzie, Angus Shaw, Archibald Norman M'Leod, SimonM'Gillivray, andGlass, 50 Hhds, assorted Glassware, and

betweenRo-

FOR SALE, No. 50, Saint Peter Street. Gloucester CHEESE, Pipe

Quebec, 29th Decr. 1813 GILBERT HENDERSON.

FOR SALEbytheSubscribers,andnowlanding

onboard the York, Wm. Cavan, master.

109 Puncheons Jamaica Spirits

All and every person or persons having claims on the a-James Hallowell, junior, of Montreal, in the Province of a quantity of assorted Hardware, &c. &c. abovedescribed land and premises, by mortgage, or other Lower-Canada, and Simon M'Gillivray, of the City of Lon- right or incumbrance, are hereby advertised to give notice don, under the Firm of M'Tavish, M'Gillivrays and Compa- thereof to the said Sheriff, at his Office, in the City of, ceased, and expired on the Thirtieth Day November Montreal, according to Law; and further that no opposition shall, according to the articles of Co-partnership afin d'annuller or afin de distraire, the whole or any part of the said land and premises, or afin de charge or servitudeon the same, will be received by the said Sheriff, during the fifteen days previous to the sale thereof. Sheriff's Office, 25d December, 1819. virtue

to wit:

PUBLIC NOTICE is hereby given, that the concern and business, formerly carried on, by the above mentioned house, will hence-forthbe transacted by the said William McGillivray, the saidArchibald NormanM'Leod, the said Simon M'Gilli vray, and John MTavish, under the former firm of MTAVISH, MGILLIVRAYS & Co. Montreal, Ist. December, 1815. YORKRose Butter, Cheshire Cheese,

GEORGETHE THIRD, by the Grace of God, MA Based out of His Majesty's Court of King'sof the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland, Bench, holding civil pleas, in and for the District of Montreal Beef, Pork, Hams, Tongues, &c.

Jamaica Ruin and Coffee,

Earthenware, Glassware, Cariole, Wheel and Nail Iron, Cordage, Hyson, Singlo, and Souchong Tea, A few of Pins and Thread," Hardware-Cross Cut and Hand Saws, Maryland Tobacco, Military Shoes and Hose, For Sale, Apply to 16th December, 1819.

ON SALE.

LIVERPOOL Salt,

Earthenware assorted, Spanish Red Wine, Port Wine,

Real old Cogniac Brandy, Strong Jamaica Rum, Claret,

Writing and Blotting Paper,

Calf and Kip Leather,

Leather Portmanteaus,

JAMES HEATH.

King, Defender of the Faith: To ourmuch beloved and faith-aforesaid, at the suit of Alexander Henry, of Montreal, Auctio-faithfulthe Legislative Councillors of our Province of Lower-Ca- nada, and to our faithful and well beloved the Knights, Citi-againstthe lands and tenements of Antoine Badel dit Du- fort, of the City and District of Montreal, trader, to me direc- zens and Burgesses of our said Province, Greeting-Whereas ted; I have seized and taken in execution as belonging to the the meeting of our Provincial Parliament stands prorogued to said ANTOINE BADEL DIT DUFORT.-1. A lot of ground the Thirty-firstDay of December instant, nevertheless for certain causes and considerations, we have thought fit further emplacement, situated in the third Cross Street, in the Saint Lawrence Suburbs, of Montreal aforesaid, bounded in the front to prorogue the same to the Thirteenth Day of January by the said Street, in the rear by Joseph Vallée, on the northnext, so that you nor any of you on the said Thirty-first east side, by the heirs of the late, the Honorable Arthur Di-vidsonof December, at our City of Quebec to appear are to be vidson, and on the south-west side, in part by a lot of ground, held or constrained, for we do will that you and each of you forty-feet square, herein after described, belonging to the said be as to us in this matter entirely exonerated; Commanding, Antoine Badel dit Dufors, and in part by the heirs of Lebrun, and by the tenor of these Presents firmlyenjoining you, and containing forty feet in front on the said Street, by forty feet every of you, and all others in this behalf interested, that on in depth, and from thence eighty feet in width, by ninety feet the said Thirteenth Day of January next, at our City of Que-further in depth, forming one hundred and thirty feet in depth bec, personally you be and appear, FORTHE DISPATCH OF SU- on the line adjoining the said heirs of the late Arthur Davidson, SINESS, to treat, do, act and conclude upon those things which in and ninetyfeet in depth on the line adjoining the said heirs Le- brunProvincial Parliament by the Common Council of our brun, from the extremity of the said lot, to the said lot of ground said Province, by the favour of God, may be ordained. In Tes-herein after described, with a house thereon erected.-2. Abo-timony whereof, these our Letters we have caused to be made ther lot of ground or emplacement situated in the said third Patent, and the Great Seal of our said Province to be thereunto Cross Street, in the Saint Lawrence Suburbs of Montreal afore-affixed-Witness, our tru-ty and well beloved SIR GEORGE said, containing forty feet in front by forty feet in depth, PAWVOST, Baronet, Captain General and Governor in Chief of bounded in the front by the said street, in the rear, and on our said Province of Lower-Canada, &c. &c &c. at the Castle one side to the north east, by the lot of ground above des-of St. Lewis, in our City of Quebec, and the Province aforesaid, described, and on the other side to the south west, by the saidthe Twenty-first day of December in the year of our Lord one Heirs of Lebrun, with a house and stable thereon erected; the thousand eight hundred and thirteen, and in the fifty-fourth said last mentioned lot of ground and premises, to be sold re- year of our Reign serving to Charlotte Contois, widow St. Jean, the possession and enjoyment thereof during her life. Now I do hereby give notice, that the saidlots of ground or emplacements and premises will be sold and adjudged to the highest bidder, at the SECOND day of MAY next, at TEN of the Clock in the forenoon, at which time and place the conditions of sale will be made known. FREDK. W. ERMATINGER, Sheriff. TRUNK marked triangle A, M. 45, containing La- All and every person or persons having claims on the above diesSlippers—-was landed on Messrs, J. W. Woolsey& described lots of ground or emplacements and premises, by mort-Co's Wharf, from on board the Britannia Jno, Storrat, from gage or other right or incumbrance, are hereby advertised to give notice thereof to the said Sheriff, at his Office aforesaid, Tenders will be received at this Office on or before the according to Law; and further that no oppositionafin d'annuller 25th January, for the whole, or part of the above supply in or afin de distraire, the whole or any part of the said lots of quantities not less than Fifty Barrels.-Security will be re-ground or emplacements and premises, or afin de charge of ser- quired. vitude on the same, will be received by the said Sheriff, during CommissaryGeneral's Office, the fifteen days previous to the sale thereof. Sheriff's Office, 23d December, 1813.

HERMAN W. RYLAND, C. C in Ch.

G. P

WANTEDTHREE THOUSAND BARRELS PRIME OR my Office in the City of Montrealafore-d, on MONDAY

GOVERNMENT CONTRACT. WANTEDfor the supply of His Majesty's Forces, MESS PORK, to be delivered at the King's Stores in MONTREAL, on or before the 15th June next

The Pork to be cured, packed, and inspected in the man- ner directed by Law, the bungs covered with Tin, brand ed with the Initialsof the furnisher, and the letter W underneath, warranted to keep good and sound for Twelve Months after delivery.

Quebec, 25th December, 1}

NOTICE

SUBSCRIPTION has been opened for the Reliefof the

MONTREAL BYvirtue of a WRIT of EXECUTION, BY wit: issued out of His Majesty's Court of King's A Widows and Orphans of Soldiers who have fallen in Bench, holding civil pleas, in and for the District of Montreal aforesaid, at the suit of Felix Souligny, of Montreal, Merchant, against the lands and tenements of James M Callum, of the Ci-ty of Quebec, in the District of Quebec, now of the City of Montreal, in the District of Montreal, to me directed; I have

Canada, in the service of their Country, and also for the Wives and Children, now at Quebec, of Soldiers absent on duty, who at this inclement Season are suffering from want of comfortable Clothing, and such other supplies as Sick- Des and Infancy may require.

A Subscription Paperhasbeen left, and subscription willtaken in execution as belonging to the said JA MESwillbereceivedattheOfficeoftheMilitarySecretary;Charity is intended to be Administered in essential Articles of comfort, and not in Money.

Soup in Boxes,

10 Pipes strong CogniacBrandy,

12 Pipes Port Wine,

80 Hogsheads 1 Teneriffe,

sugars,

chocolate,

13 Cases Irish Linens and Cotton Shirtings ALSO IN STORE,Copper bolts,bar iron,Muscovado few casks whitening and pipe clay. Quebec, 12th Novr. 1815. 1. W. WOOLSEY & Co. FOR SALE,received by the Subscriber, by the late

rivals from London and Liverpool, and for Sale at his Store on St. Andrew's Wharf; best superfine Navy blue,black, bottle and mixed cloths, ditto military grey, Brus els carpeting, elegant patterns; common ditto, do do. Beavver and other gloves, best White Chapel needles, darning do. sail knittingdo. best cast steel Kirby fish hooks, a few elegant Silver Epaulets. He has on hand Eau de Cologne in boxesof 6 bottles, 4 barrels cream of tarter, a few barrels. of white boiling peas, real Coignac brandy in small lots, chamber and blacksmith coals, La. DELAMARE.

Quebec, 4th Novr. 1815.

FORSale by FRANCOIS QUIROUET, at. Pert

FOR

200 Chaldron best New Castle coals,

50 Kegs white paint, 40 Kegs green paint, blue, &c.

15 Crates soup plates,

8 Hogsheads Herefordshire cyder,

15 Casks assorted cut glass ware,

Sacks fresh hops,

10 Puncheonsstrong Jamaica spirits, which he will

dispose of lowfor cash. Quebec, 3d Novr. 1813.

NOW

Linen, coarse and fine, in pieces from 6 yards each sugar, coffee in tierces, and two hhds first quality James

to 50, &c.

Linen Shirts, coarse and fine.

Linen and Cotton Thread, white and colored. Table Linen,

Harness, Saddles and Bridles,

Boots, and patent Boot Tops.

Superfine and fine Coats and Waistcoats, Stocking web Pantaloons.

St Peter Street, Decr. 1, 1813 W. MAGEE, Jr. & Co. MISSING

Greenock.

Whoever may have the said Trunk, are requested to deliver to the Subscriber, who will pay all reasonable charges since being landed. 22d Decr. 1813

T

M. CAMPBELL,

HE Term of Partnership of W. HendersonCo, of Quebec, and Henderson, Armour & Co. of Montreal, expired the 1st ultimo. Mr. ROBERTARMOUR is empower'd to settle all Accounts at Montreal, and Mr. JAMES HENDER SON at Quebec. Wm. HENDERSON, by his Attorney, JAMES HENDERSON. ROBERT ARMOUR, JAMES HENDERSON.

Quebec, 1st Decr. 1813,

THESUBSCRIBER HAS FOR SALE: Pipes SuperiorOld PORT WINE, 10 M. Standard STAVES, And a Few Tonsof COALS. Quebec, 1st Dec. 1819. M. CAMPBELL. MOORE'S BOARDING HOUSE, Cul-de SacStreet, No. 5.-Three or four Gentlemen may be genteely accomo-

and taken in execution as belonging to the said JAMES being in the Township of Sherrington, in the said District of Montreal, to wit: lotsnumbers six, seven, eight, ten, eleven, and thirteen, in the fourth range of lots in the said Township Quebec, 28th December, 1813. of Sherrington; lots numbers six, eight, nine, ten, twelve, thir-teen, fifteen, sixteen, seventeen, nineteen, and twenty, in the MONTREAL BYvirtue of a WRIT of EXECUTION is Bench, holding civil pleas, in and for the District of Mon- numbers seven, eight, nine, eleven, twelve, four-teen, fifteen, treal, aforesaid, at the suit of Patrick Phelan, of Montreal, sixteen , eighteen and nineteen, in the sixth range of lots in the in the said district, yeoman, against the lands and tenements said Township of Sherrington; lots number eight, nine, ten, of Samuel Borriadge, late of Latortue in the Seigniory of twelve, thirteen, fifteen, sixteen and nineteen, in the seventh Lasalle, in the said district, yeoman, to me directed; I have range of lots in the said Township of Sherrington; and lot num- seized and taken in execution as belonging to the said SAM- ber seventeen in the said seventh range of lots, with the excep UEL BURRIADGE1. A lot of land situated tion of seven acres in the north-easterly corner thereof. Now in the and SeignioryofLasalle, and formerly making part of I do hereby give notice, that the said lots of land; will be sold the domain thereof, in the parish of Saint Philip, in the said and adjudged to the highest bidder, at my Office, in the City district, containing three arpents in front by thirty arpents in of Montreal aforesaid, onMONDAY the SECOND day of depth, bounded in the front by the river Latortue, MAYnext,atELEVENoftheClockintheforenoon,atrear by the lands of Saint André, on one side to the north which time and place the conditions of sale will he made known. east by Daniel Stewart, and on the other side by the land next hereinafter described, with a stone house, barn, stable and other buildings thereon erected-2. A lot of land si tuated in the said parish of Saint Philip, adjoining the land above described, containing one arpentin front, by about thirty- nine arpents in depth, bounded in the front by the said riv- er Latortue, in the rear by the said lands of Saint André, on one side by the said land above described, and on the -o- ther side by Mr. Fuller-3. A lot of land commonly cal led a woodlot, situated in the said parish of Saint Philip, con- taining three arpents in front, by about twenty-six arpents indepth, bounded in the front by Henry Le Claire, in the rear by the lands of Latortue, on one side by Jean Baptise Rouillé, dit Commercy, and on the other side by Alexis La Cavée, dit Hamelin. Now I do hereby give notice that the said lots of land and premises will be sold and adjudged to the highest bidder, at the church door of the parish of Saint Philip, a-foresaid, on MONDAY the SECOND day of MAY next, atTEN of the clock in the forenoon, at which time and place the conditions of sale will be made known.

TOWIT. BYissuedout of His Majesty's Court of King's Bench,holdingcivilpleas,in andsaid theDistrictSherrington; lots dated with boarding and lodging on reasonable term

FREDE. W. ERMATINGER, Sheriff.

FREDE, W. ERMATINGER, Sheriff.

J. MOORE returns thanks to his friends and the public, for the very liberal encouragement he has hitherto received in his line of business as a TAYLOR, and hopes by his future exertions to merit their approbation.

B

lately

Quebec, 23d. December, 1815. COLTMANS & HALEHAVE FOR SALE, 1, St. Peter's

Street, the Sails, Rigging, and other Stores, complete, belonging to the Brig Nancy, about 150 tons register.

A patent Windlass for a vessel of 300 to 400 tubs, A Set composition Rudder Ironsdo.

2 Tone LignumVita,

2

Oakum,

1

Round and square Iron,Copper Bolts and Nails,

2

All and every person or persons having claims on the above described lots of land, by mortgage or other right or incum brance, are hereby advertised to give notice thereof to the said Sheriff, at his Office aforesaid, according to Law; and further that no opposition afin d'annuller or afin de distraire, the whole or any part of the said lots of land, or afin de charge or servitudedry other articles adapted to Ship building. on the same, will be received by the said Sheriff, during the fifteen days previous to the sale thereof. Sheriff's Office, 23d December, 1813. Byvirtue of MONTREAL 2

Round and square Iron,

CapstanSpindle, Windlass Necks,

A Set of Pump Geer, Blocks of various sizes, and in-

TO WIT

ALSO

A few pipes Port Wine,

Quebec, 9th Decr. 1813.

FOR SALE BY THE SUBSCRIBERS

issuedour of His Majesty's Court of ONE Hundredand fiftyPuncheonsGrenada and St. Vin-

King's Bench, holding civil pleas, in and for the District of Montreal, aforesaid, at the suit of Pierre Berthelet, of the City of Montreal, in the said district, merchant, against the lands and tenements of the vacant succession of Amos Brown- son, Junior, deceased, in his life time of the same place, Tra- der, in the hands of Joseph Desautels, also of the same place, Public Notary, as Curator duly appointed to the said vacant succession, to me directed; I have seized and taken in exe- cution as being lands and tenements belonging to the said va- All and every personsor persons having claims onthe a--bovesuccession of the said AMOS BROWNSON, Junior, bove described lots of land and premises, by mortgage, or otherin the hands of the said Joseph Desautels, as Curator as a-ther right or incumbrance, are hereby advertised to give noticethereoftothethat certain piece of ground or emplacement tice thereof to the said Sheriff, at his Office, in the City of situated in Notre Dame Street, in the City of Montreal, a Montreal, according to Law; and further that no opposition foresaid, containing in front, fifty feet, by onehundred and afind'annuller or afin de distraire, the whole or any part of the said lots of land and premises, or afin de charge or servi- tudeon the same, will be received by the said Sheriff dur- ing the fifteen days previous to the sale thereof, Sheriff's Office, 23d December, 1815.

BYvirtue of a WRIT of EXECUTION

eighty feet in depth, without guarantee as to the exact mea sure, bounded in the front by the said street Notre Dame, in the rear by the glacis, (with reserve of the King's rights) joining on one side to Alexander Henry, Esquire, and onthe other side, to Dominique Rousseau, with a two story sto house thereon erected, and the right of passagein the Vin-

cent Rum

3) do. very strong Jamaica Spirits,

20 hds, and 50 barrels Muscovado Sugar, 9 do. Loaf Sugar,

16 cases superior Claret, in not less than 2 doz, Few doz. Martinique Liqueurs, 300 French Burr-Stones,

20 bags good old Coffee, 6 cases of Soap,

150 pair of White Ash Oars, squared & newready made, Large Table and West-India Cod Fish,

Puncheons Leeward Island Rum, ten bbls. muscovado CHAS. F. AYLWIN.

River Leaf Tobacco.

Nov. 3, 1815.

A

FOR SALE,

CHOICE Assortment of 4-4 and 7-8 Irish LINENS, ANDW. MOIR Queen's Wharf. Quebec, 19th August, 1815. Province of Lower Canada, IN THE KING'S BENCH. District of Quebec.

WEDNESDAY, 20th Octr. 1813,

ORDERED, that all persons having any claimsor demands against the said WILLIAM THOMP sox, the elder, do fyle the same in the Office of the Prothonotaries of this Court, on or before the First day of February next, with such proofs thereof, as they may be advised, for such proceedings thereon, as to Law and Justice may appertain. PERRAULT & ROSS, P. B. R.

DONALD GRANT PlaintiffGEORGE STANLEY of the City of Quebec, Boot and Shoe-maker, Curator, duly appointed in Law to WILLIAM THOMPSON the elder of the same Cityof Quebec, Tavern- Keeper, interdicted for cause of insanity. Province of Lower-Canada, District of Quebec.

GEORGE GLENNY,

IN THE KING'S BENCH. WEDNESDAY, 20th Octr. 1815

Plaintiffhaving claims or demands

THOMAS WHITE Cura-gainst the vacant Estate and suctur to the vacant Estate cession of the said late JAMES and succession of JAMES GLENNY, do fyle the same in the GLENNY, late of the City Office of the Prothonotaries of ofQuebec, Merchant. this Court, on or before the First day of February next, with suchproofs thereof, as they may be ad- vised, for such proceedings thereon, as to Law and Justice may appertain

NOW

Defendant.

By the Court,

PERRAULT & ROSS, P. B. R.B. R.

Now landing from Brig Cassicks, and for sale by the

Subscriber.

da

130 Puncheons strong and fine favored Jamaica Ram, 25 Do. high proof Leewards do 15 Pipes Sicilian Wine-otherwise called Bronti Madeira, 10 Hhds. fine York and James River Tobacco, 60 Baskets fine old Cheshire & Glouster Cheese, 54 Crates of assorted Earthen Ware.

A few Casks 16 Board, & Covering Nails,

An assortment of Boys Shoes,

Black Lead Ground,

140 Ton white Liverpool Salt.

7 ChaldronsCanal Coal

ALSO, IN STORE

A Choice Assortment of Ribbons, Sewing Silks, and Twist Men'sfine Beaver and silk Hats,

Boiled Linseed Oil in Hhds, and Jars, Whiting, Crown Blue, Cordage from 12 thread to 9 inch.

Bar Iron.-Five casks assorted Glass Ware.

60Barrels picked Salmon and 50 Barrelspickled Herrings Quebec, 1st Nov. 1813. GEORGE SYMES,

NOW LANDING FROM SALUS, & FOR SALE, 80TiercesRice,

50 Tons Blacksmiths Coals,

5 Boxes Cambric Muslins, and a few packages HouseholdFurniture,

ALSO-Landing out of BrigsALEXENDR& DISPATCH-

& Boxes S. G.wCotton Shirting,

20 Cases fancy printed Calicous,

3 Bales mix'd Kerseymeres,

3 Bales white Flannels.

The whole of which will be sold low for Cash, or at short GEO SYMES

10 dozen best Florence Oil, and a few bushelsbest boi-lingPease...ForCashorShortCredit.

Quebec, 9th Decr. 1815.

BREHAUT & SHEPPARD,

FOR SALE, SPANISHWine in pipes,

Ditto in pipes,

Port Wine bottled, in casks of 6 dozen, Duttoin pipes,

Hollands Gin of a superior quality,

Coniac Brandy,

Jamaica Spirits,

Leaf Tobacco,

Refined Sugar,

Window Glass 1-2 by 7-1-2,

Irish Linens,

Quebec, 5d. Nov, 1813.

JUSTARRIVED per Ship ROBERT, and for Sale by theSubscribers, Four Bales real Superfine, Blue, Black, brown and mix'tCloth, also commonditto of different colours, at a reduced price for Cash or approved Credit-also aSuit of Sails for a Schooner of 80 tons. BREHAUT & SHEPPARD. Quebec, 4th Nov, 1913.

E

FOR SALE,

EIGHTThousandfeetMerchantable Oak Timber, 12,000 feet ship building and Oak Culls, of a good qua-lity.

7,000 feet Merchantable Pine Timber.

Apply to JONES, WHITE & MELVIN Quebec, 231 Septr. 1813.

A few firkins Irish Butter, first quality. ROGERSON, HUNTER & Co, GEORGE BROWNE (removedto No. 9, St. George Street, Quebec, 25th Nov. 1815 No, 8, St. Peter Street. Upper Town)has for sale.-Pink and White sparkling Champaigne, Royal Soutern, Claret, Malmsey Madeira, Lon-NOTICE-All persons having any legal demandagainsttheEstateofMr.JoanTraill,formerlyofLiverpool,inEngland,andlateTraisQuebec,Merchant,deceased,areherebyrequiredbytheExecutors,toproducethesamedulyattested,best Old Port,

MONTREAL BY to wit. sued out of His Majesty's Court of King'sBench,holdingcivilpleas,inandforthedistrictofMon-treal,aforesaid,atthesuitofFrançoisMenetre,district theLake-Francis,intheof the gable ends, or murs mitoyes, as the treal, aforesaid, at the suit of François Metotte, of the same shall belong and appertain to the above described pre- mises, the whole as the said premises now are, and may be Lake Saint Francis, in the Parish of Soulanges, yeoman, found to extend, with all and every the rights, members and andMarie LouisePoirier, his wife, against the lands and te- appurtenances thereunto belonging. And also the following nements of Clement Racicot, of the Parish of Vaudreuil, in lots of land situate, lying and being in the Township of Eck-the said district, merchant, and George Hamel, widow of EtienneCouturier, late of the parish of Soulanges, in the said ingham, in the said district, that is to say, the lot numbertwenty-five, in the first range; the lot number twenty fivein district, yeoman, as commune en biens, with the said Etienne Couturierthesecondrangethelotsnumbertwo, twenty-four, Couturier, and natural Tutrixto her minor children, to me and twenty-five, in the third range; and the lot numberdirected; I have seized and taken in execution as belonging to the mid GEORGE HAMEL, in her quality aforesaid, twenty-three, in the fourth range of lots in the said Town England, and late of Quebec, Merchant, deceased, are hereby requiredparticular and London market Madeira in casks and ship of Buckingham. Now I do hereby give notice that the required by the Executors, to produce the sameduly attested,andallthosewhoareindebtedtothesaidEstate,arerequestedtoa land situated and being at the Petite cotede Vaudreuil,infrontbytwenty-eightarpentsmoreorless,indepth,adjudged to the within twelve months from this date,foradjustment;—AndallthosewhoareindebtedtothesaidEstate,arerequestedtomakeimmediatethe parish of Vaudreuil,aforesaid,containingtwoarpentsmoreorless,indepth,onthenortheastsideofthepublicroadofthesaidcote,andrequested to Porter, Burton Ale, real CogniacBrandy and Holland's Ge- in front by twenty-eight arpents more or less, in depth, aforesaid, on TUESDAY the THIRD day of MAY next, at make immediate payment to neva, a few dozen choice old East-India Arrack, Jamaica Spi- the north east side of the public road of the said cote, and ELEVEN of the Clock in the forenoon, at which time and rits and MartinicoNoyeau.three arpents in front, by fifteen arpents more or less, in depth, on the south west side of the said road, the whole place the conditions of sale will be made known. FREDK. W. ERMATINGER , Sheriff. being bounded in the front by Dominique Charlebois, and All and every person or persons having claims on the lands Gabriel Saint Denis, in the rear by Jean Baptiste Joubert and tenements above described, by mortgage or other right on one side in part by the end of the lands at the upper and or incumbrance, are hereby advertised to give notice thereof ALL persons having accounts ordemands against the Subs-Queen's ware,inCrates,eachcontainingacompletedinnerservice,writingpaperofvarioussorts,PatentShot,Shot,beltsandPowderFlasks,afewpairslargedouble,roseblankets,trunkbestLondonmadeboots,&c.&c..pault,andontheothersidebyChevrier,thefather,withandfurtherthatnooppositionafind'annullerorafindedistrairethewhole,oranypartofthesaidlandsandtenementsorafisdechargeorarresteonthesame,willbereceivedbythesaidSheriffduringthefifteendaysprevioustosaidlands and tenements or to settle their respective accounts, as after this notice all ac-that the said land and premises will be sold and adjudged to dechargeorservitudeonthesame,willbereceivedbythesaidcounts which have been of more than a year's standing, will be the highest bidder, at the Church door of the parish of Vaudreuil,aforesaid,onthe fifteen days previous to the sale there-indiscriminately atthesaidSheriff'sOffice,2ndDecember, 1813.Vaudreuil, aforesaid, on MONDAY the SECOND day of saidSheriff's Office, 234 December, 1815.

JAMES IRVINE, THO WILSON,Executors.

Quebec, 21st October, 1813. NOTICE

ALSO...double and single refined and clay'd Sugar, West India Coffee, Florente Oil in cases, containing 12 bottles each, white wine Vinegar in Jars, of 54 to 6 gallous, Wedgwood's

othersare hereby notified to produce them for imme-service, writing paper of various morts Patent Shot, Shot

HALL & GOWEN.

belts and Powder Flasks, a few pairs large dble, rows blankets, trunk best London made boots, &c. &c.

N. B. The Wines, Brandy &c. may be bad in quantitiesnot less than two, the Porter &c. not less than three dozens Quebec, 13th May, 1815.
